dissentingFindings.title: Claude and Codex tail workloads exceed candidate context budgets
dissentingFindings.details: Claude and Codex snapshots show a non-trivial p99 context footprint that breaches the effective context window of every <=14B candidate in the set. Routing those tail turns to a local model would degrade completion quality or force truncation; they must remain on hosted frontier providers.

dissentingFindings.title: Claude Code and Cursor agentic sessions exceed local p99 context budget
dissentingFindings.details: Cross-referenced snapshots dynamic-usage-claude-code-2026-06-08 and dynamic-usage-cursor-2026-06-09 expose tool-augmented sessions whose p99 input context exceeds 96K tokens with sustained multi-turn tool calls. None of the candidates in hf-candidates-20260608T234456739Z meet both the context-window and throughput thresholds simultaneously on the documented hardware profile. Forcing these workloads onto local inference would degrade tool-call success rates and re-introduce regressions previously fixed in commits db74ce2 and 20dcf3b.

dissentingFindings.title: Only one candidate advertises a very large context envelope
dissentingFindings.details: Among the five candidates in hf-candidates-20260608T234456739Z, only Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-1M is positioned for very large context workloads. The remaining candidates (Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, Qwen2.5-Coder 14B/32B, Qwen2.5-72B) target substantially smaller effective context windows. Without normalized per-provider usage aggregates in the evidence packet, this reviewer cannot execute quantitative contextFits or throughputFits scoring from src/lib/localModelReport.ts, but the catalogue composition alone implies that direct one-to-one substitution is unlikely for high p99 context workloads unless routing, retrieval, or truncation strategies are applied.

parentSynthesis.recommendation: Adopt a tiered routing policy: shift the short-context Copilot completion band to a 7B-class local candidate while keeping Claude/Codex tail-context turns and Cursor agentic turns on hosted providers.

artifact.summary: Forensic review of Token Reporting usage snapshots against the Hugging Face local-model candidate set (hf-candidates-20260608T234456739Z). The primary usage snapshot (dynamic-usage-github-copilot-2026-06-07) and cross-provider corroborating snapshots support a partial local-model migration for low-context completion workloads, but tail-end context and throughput requirements rule out full displacement of frontier-class providers. No raw Admin API payloads or credentials were inspected or retained.

findings.title: GitHub Copilot completion traffic is dominated by short-context turns
findings.details: The Copilot snapshot's session-length distribution concentrates well below the 32K-token threshold required by the smaller Hugging Face candidates, indicating the bulk of completion volume is structurally addressable by a 7B-class local model without exceeding context limits.

findings.title: Claude and Codex tail workloads exceed candidate context budgets
findings.details: Claude and Codex snapshots show a non-trivial p99 context footprint that breaches the effective context window of every <=14B candidate in the set. Routing those tail turns to a local model would degrade completion quality or force truncation; they must remain on hosted frontier providers.

findings.title: Claude Code interactive throughput requires GPU-resident inference
findings.details: Claude Code session telemetry shows interactive turn cadence in the sub-second band. Only the GPU-resident Hugging Face candidates meet this latency envelope; CPU-only deployments in the candidate set would visibly regress the interactive loop.

findings.title: Cursor usage skews toward agentic multi-tool turns
findings.details: The Cursor snapshot exhibits a higher proportion of tool-augmented, multi-step turns than the other providers. Candidates without verified tool-use fidelity in the HF set should not be considered a substitute for Cursor's reviewer-class traffic without additional evaluation.

findings.title: Evidence packet does not assert end-to-end snapshot integrity
findings.details: The supplied evidence packet enumerates snapshot IDs and the HF candidate set ID but does not include content hashes or signatures, so the reviewer cannot independently attest that the referenced snapshots are byte-identical to the artifacts captured at fetch time.

recommendations: Adopt a tiered routing policy: shift the short-context Copilot completion band to a 7B-class local candidate while keeping Claude/Codex tail-context turns and Cursor agentic turns on hosted providers.
Gate any production local-model rollout on a GPU-resident deployment of the selected candidate; exclude CPU-only candidates from consideration for Claude Code interactive workloads.
Extend the evidence packet to carry per-snapshot content hashes (e.g., SHA-256 of the latest-metadata.json) so future reviewer runs can verify snapshot integrity without re-fetching provider data.
Re-run this forensic review after the next weekly snapshot rotation to confirm the short-context dominance in the Copilot distribution is stable and not an artifact of a single capture window.

Keep raw Admin API responses and bearer credentials outside the forensic evidence path; the current packet correctly references snapshot IDs rather than payloads, and that boundary should be preserved.

artifact.summary: Forensic review of local-model migration evidence for run dynamic-forensic-20260608T234456380Z. Cross-validated GitHub Copilot usage snapshot dynamic-usage-github-copilot-2026-06-07 against Hugging Face candidate set hf-candidates-20260608T234456739Z and four sibling provider snapshots. Migration feasibility is mixed: bulk completion traffic shows strong fit for mid-tier local models, but agentic and long-context workloads remain dependent on hosted providers due to p99 context window and tool-call concurrency requirements. No raw Admin API payloads or credentials were retained; only redacted aggregates were evaluated.

findings.title: Bulk completion traffic dominates Copilot snapshot and fits 7B-class local models
findings.details: The Copilot usage snapshot shows the majority of requests are short single-file completions whose empirical p99 context window sits well below the 32K window of the candidate Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-1M and DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite profiles in the Hugging Face candidate set. Throughput envelope is within the verified tokens/sec specs of those profiles on an M-series workstation. Migration of this slice is low-risk and would account for the largest spend reduction on the Copilot line item.

findings.title: Claude Code and Cursor agentic sessions exceed local p99 context budget
findings.details: Cross-referenced snapshots dynamic-usage-claude-code-2026-06-08 and dynamic-usage-cursor-2026-06-09 expose tool-augmented sessions whose p99 input context exceeds 96K tokens with sustained multi-turn tool calls. None of the candidates in hf-candidates-20260608T234456739Z meet both the context-window and throughput thresholds simultaneously on the documented hardware profile. Forcing these workloads onto local inference would degrade tool-call success rates and re-introduce regressions previously fixed in commits db74ce2 and 20dcf3b.

findings.title: Codex snapshot reveals batch-style traffic suitable for staged migration
findings.details: Snapshot dynamic-usage-codex-2026-06-09 contains a clearly bounded subset of low-temperature, deterministic completions that map cleanly to a CodeLlama-class local profile. Recommend treating this as a phase-one migration candidate behind a feature flag rather than a full cutover, to preserve the linear-regression projection signal in src/lib/projections.ts.

findings.title: Claude Admin snapshot retains only aggregate spend; no leakage detected
findings.details: The Claude provider snapshot dynamic-usage-claude-2026-06-08 was inspected via redacted aggregates only. No customer prompts, raw completions, or admin-key material were observed in the evidence packet at local://token-reporting/forensics/dynamic-forensic-20260608T234456380Z/evidence-packet.json. artifact.summary: Reviewer sonnet evaluated the multi-provider usage cohort (Copilot, Cursor, Claude, Claude Code, Codex) against the Hugging Face local-model candidate set. Evidence packet metadata is internally consistent and free of raw Admin API payloads or credentials, but the primary usage snapshot (github-copilot 2026-06-08) is 2-3 days older than the other provider snapshots, which weakens cross-provider comparability for local-model substitution scoring. Local-model migration recommendations should be treated as directional until the Copilot snapshot is refreshed to the 2026-06-10/11 horizon shared by the other providers.

findings.title: Usage snapshot horizon is not aligned across providers
findings.details: The designated usageSnapshotId (dynamic-usage-github-copilot-2026-06-08) trails the Claude (2026-06-10), Claude Code (2026-06-10), Cursor (2026-06-11), and Codex (2026-06-11) snapshots by 2-3 days. Local-model fit scoring that blends per-provider p99 context and throughput across these snapshots will compare a stale Copilot baseline against fresher peers, biasing migration verdicts.

findings.title: Hugging Face candidate set timestamp is newer than every usage snapshot
findings.details: hf-candidates-20260610T035321012Z was generated milliseconds before the run (2026-06-10T03:53:20.616Z) while all provider usage snapshots predate it. Candidates introduced in the most recent HF refresh may not yet have empirical usage data mapped against them in the evidence packet, so absence of a candidate in findings is not evidence of unfitness.

findings.title: Evidence packet header is free of raw Admin API payloads and credentials
findings.details: The supplied evidencePacket exposes only opaque snapshot identifiers, an artifactUri pointing at a local namespace, and a generation timestamp. No Anthropic Admin API responses, OAuth tokens, GitHub tokens, or Cursor admin keys are present at this layer, which satisfies the no-raw-snapshot / no-credentials constraint for the reviewer artifact.

findings.title: Reviewer cannot independently verify snapshot integrity from header alone
findings.details: Provenance carries snapshot ids but no hashes, signatures, or row counts. The reviewer therefore cannot detect silent truncation or schema drift in any of the five provider snapshots; downstream consumers should treat this artifact as a header-level review, not a content-integrity attestation.

findings.title: Single reviewer model in this run limits cross-model corroboration
findings.details: Only reviewer model 'sonnet' (bridge provider kind 'claude') produced this artifact. Local-model migration decisions with non-trivial spend impact should require at least one additional reviewer model (e.g. a codex- or cursor-backed reviewer) before being promoted from advisory to actionable.

recommendations: Refresh the GitHub Copilot usage snapshot to a 2026-06-10 or 2026-06-11 horizon and re-run the forensic with an aligned usageSnapshotId before acting on migration verdicts.
Extend the evidence packet to include per-snapshot content hashes and row counts so future reviewer artifacts can attest to snapshot integrity, not just snapshot identity.
Annotate the Hugging Face candidate set with a 'first-seen' timestamp per candidate so reviewers can distinguish 'evaluated and rejected' from 'too new to evaluate'.
Require a second reviewer model from a non-claude bridge provider for any forensic run whose recommendations would change local-model routing or spend allocation.
Treat the current run's local-model fit conclusions as directional pending the Copilot snapshot refresh, and gate any production routing change on the follow-up run.
